title: Trace slow and error spans in Explore

blocks.content:
Traces connect user-visible latency to which span consumed the time. You already fixed the incident window in UTC; now you locate representative error and slow paths.

To query traces, complete the following steps.

1. In the main Grafana navigation on the left, open **Explore**. If the navigation is collapsed, open it with the menu control, then choose **Explore**. Select your Tempo data source (for example `grafanacloud-demoinfra-traces`).

1. Apply the same absolute time range as metrics and logs.

Run:

```traceql
{ status = error }
```

Note how many traces match and which span names recur.

Then narrow to slow error spans:

```traceql
{ span:duration > 200ms && status = error }
```

Open one trace into the waterfall view and record:

- Root span name and total duration.
- Which child span consumed the most time.
- Roughly what percentage of total trace time that child represents.

Run:

```traceql
{ resource.service.name = "quickpizza" && span:duration > 200ms }
```

If the resource attribute differs in your stack, substitute the value your services use.

Reflection: The root span duration should often be in the same ballpark as k6's HTTP request timing. If it diverges, what explanations are plausible (client-side timing, connection reuse, redirects, missing instrumentation, clock skew)?

Check your work: you can move on when the time range still matches your k6 window, you opened at least one error or slow trace, you wrote down root span duration, dominant child span, and rough share of trace time, and you wrote a short answer to the reflection prompt. If TraceQL returns nothing, confirm the Tempo data source, service labels, and window, then open the troubleshooting topics for this milestone.

In the next milestone you combine your notes into a postmortem-style narrative and complete the reflection list.
